items.title: Oracle Surges 12% While Apple, Amazon Tumble in Choppy Tech Week
items.description: Tech stocks delivered a choppy week, with the sector’s biggest names diverging sharply as AI spending debates intensified and bear market territory claimed two Magnificent Seven members. Incredibly, every single Magnificent 7 stock is now down year-to-date after NVIDIA (NASDAQ: NVDA) shares slipped 2.23% on Friday. Let’s dive into the biggest storylines in technology this week. While many stocks ... Oracle Surges 12% While Apple, Amazon Tumble in Choppy Tech Week

items.title: Apple (AAPL) Trading 10% Below Analyst Targets After This Week’s 8% Drop
items.description: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) closed at $255.78 on February 13, down 7.95% from the previous week’s $277.86. Yet Wall Street analysts still see the stock climbing to an average target of $292.15, implying 10% upside from current levels. That disconnect raises a question: is the market pricing in risks analysts are missing, or did the selloff ... Apple (AAPL) Trading 10% Below Analyst Targets After This Week’s 8% Drop

items.title: Apple’s Siri shocker is flashing a warning AAPL bulls can’t ignore
items.description: Apple (AAPL), since WWDC 2024, has sold a simple comeback story: Siri is getting a real AI brain, further cementing Apple's AI credentials. CEO Tim Cook put it this way. However, the latest reporting presents a problem for Apple and Siri enthusiasts. The issue for Apple comes down to one word: ...

items.title: Timely Tips And Tools On How To Invest In Stocks In This Market
items.description: This week's how to invest newsletter highlights a simple three-step routine, a tool of the week and a timely tip from Bill O'Neil.

items.title: These 3 Stocks Appear to Be Set Up For a Massive 2026
items.description: It’s been quite the run in the stock market, for investors of all ages and risk profiles. However, it’s been true that investors who have continued to hold overweight exposure to growth stocks have continued to perform better than those who have been sitting in fixed income assets, cash or other lower-beta stocks.
